instruction: Please check today's temperature range for "San Francisco" and save one line to "$HOME/Desktop/weather_today_range_4_1.txt" in this format: today_temp_range: max=<value>C min=<value>C.
reference_command:
geo=$(curl -s 'https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=San+Francisco&count=1&language=en&format=json')
lat=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].latitude')
lon=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].longitude')
forecast=$(curl -s "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat}&longitude=${lon}&daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min&timezone=auto&forecast_days=1")
max_t=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.temperature_2m_max[0]')
min_t=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.temperature_2m_min[0]')
printf 'today_temp_range: max=%sC min=%sC\n' "$max_t" "$min_t" > "$HOME/Desktop/weather_today_range_4_1.txt"

parameters.expected_command:
geo=$(curl -s 'https://geocoding-api.open-meteo.com/v1/search?name=San+Francisco&count=1&language=en&format=json')
lat=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].latitude')
lon=$(echo "$geo" | jq -r '.results[0].longitude')
forecast=$(curl -s "https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast?latitude=${lat}&longitude=${lon}&daily=temperature_2m_max,temperature_2m_min&timezone=auto&forecast_days=1")
max_t=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.temperature_2m_max[0]')
min_t=$(echo "$forecast" | jq -r '.daily.temperature_2m_min[0]')
printf 'today_temp_range: max=%sC min=%sC\n' "$max_t" "$min_t"
